codeReview.summary: Commit 8a6d01a closes the remaining round-2 blocker. `RestrictedFS.lstat()` now resolves only the parent directory, re-checks ACLs on the rejoined path, and then delegates to `vfs.lstat()`, which blocks ancestor-symlink escapes without following legitimate leaf symlinks. Together with the already-reviewed sync guards and the `VfsAdapter` sync-null → async fallback, the current `statSync/readDirSync/lstatSync` and `stat/readDir/lstat` surfaces are ACL-symmetric and I did not find a remaining reachable metadata leak.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker marked `followedProcedure: true`, but the new shell case in `restricted-fs-async-lstat.test.ts` was not actually a red-phase test for the targeted bug. It invokes just-bash `stat`, which only calls `t.fs.stat()`; the handoff's pre-fix run reports `7 tests | 1 failed`, so this shell case stayed green against the buggy async `lstat()` implementation. Future guidance should either require a truly lstat-driven shell command for this class of fix or explicitly label such cases as regression-only smoke tests.

summary: Pass: the async `RestrictedFS.lstat()` fix closes the ancestor-symlink metadata leak and restores ACL symmetry across the `VfsAdapter` sync/async `stat`, `readDir`, and `lstat` surfaces. No blocking issues found.
